storyInfo: Eloysius reports to Chairman Witte after the conclusion of the Far North Incident, but does not tell him that she has already disclosed information about the Sarcophagus to Rhodes Island. News of the vein exhaustion continues to spread, and the repercussions are felt across Ursus.

attributes.content: <p=1>Veteran's Home, Deity Grypherburg</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: ...This is my report on the Far North Incident. I will submit my written report within one week.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Thank you, Lieutenant. But I still have questions.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: What you've mentioned—getting into the mining district, investigating the miners, evacuating them under the Armies' gunfire... those are merely the process.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: An event must have cause, process, and result, just like how crops grow from seeds, and yield fruits.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I apologize for my poor presentation, Your High Excellency, but I have mentioned both the cause of the event and how we handled the aftermath.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: All scattered information. You have described the details, but you have not seen the big picture. How can the reader understand what the writer fails to perceive?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Have you forgotten how to write a report in the five years since you graduated from the military academy? Or do you want me to submit a fragmented, incomplete report to His Imperial Majesty?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I can forgive your youthful errors, but Ursus cannot afford to wait.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I apologize. I will fill in the gaps in the written report—
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: We do not have the time to address the consequences of your omissions. The price of trial and error is too high.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I need you to tell me, right here, right now. What role do you think Grand Duchess Elisabeth played? Were there signs of her association with the Armies? As for the Infected that we rescued...
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Can they truly be of help to us?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I'm not sure I have sufficient information to talk about it in an informed manner...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: You experienced the events first-hand. No one is more informed than you.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Speak, child. I will listen.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: ...I believe that the Grand Duchess was simply going with the flow.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: She had learned about the Royal Guard's death and the miners' escape before we even planned to enter the mining district.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: All she needed to do was to let a little information out, and the Fourth Army would have an excuse to 'clean up', and proceed towards the Central Governorate under the pretext of quelling the rebellion.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: This way, the Grand Duchess could cover up the death of a Royal Guard on her watch, and the Army can deploy on the borders of the Central Governorate... as it does now.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: As for why the Army is doing this... I believe you know better than anyone.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: They mistake the Tsar's benevolence for weakness. They will pay the price.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I concur with most of your speculation, Elotta. But there is one thing you are missing—the Yurodstvo.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: It disappeared. I could not confirm whether it was real or not, and I do not even feel the need to dig any deeper. After all... we witnessed the burning of the last Yurodstvo with our own eyes.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: You do not believe it was the Yurodstvo who appeared in the Far North?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I simply believe you know more than I do.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: The troubling madman... a poisonous relic of an older era. I will get to the bottom of it. Ursus does not need a Yurodstvo anymore.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Now, let us get back to the topic at hand. Were their goals accomplished, Lieutenant?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: They failed to hush up news of the ore vein exhaustion. Which leads us to your other question—
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Can the Infected that we rescued truly be of help to us? In fact, they've already helped.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: The belt of torches on the ice awakened all the outposts around Lake Neva on the Grypherburg side. Every household in Ursus has heard their message.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: 'Ursus abandoned them! Who will be next?' Everyone asks the same question: the newspapers, intercity comms, late-night radio...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: The miners in the mines ask, the workers in the city ask; wealthy citizens ask, poor vagabonds ask; soldiers ask, students ask.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: The Grand Duchess expected the miners to die in the Far North. But they survived, testifying to the exhaustion of the ore veins, and exposing the inadequacy of the reforms—
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Slow, gradual improvement is not enough for the pressing needs of Ursus's development.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: We must act, before even more people are fed into the hungry machine of the Empire.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: You said yourself that Ursus can no longer afford to wait...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: You're digressing, Lieutenant.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: You said Rhodes Island helped the Infected miners send the message.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Yes. Right now, however, we should focus on—
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: What do you think of Rhodes Island?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: ...We should be vigilant.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: They demonstrated military-level coordination during the tracking and evacuation process, and they possess a natural advantage in their understanding of the Infected. There are plenty of reasons to be vigilant.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Or perhaps, their strength is something that we can leverage.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: As far as I am aware, Rhodes Island has no permanent presence in Ursus. Perhaps we can invite them to set up a branch office in Grypherburg or a nearby city...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Perhaps.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: The Grand Duchess might strike first.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: She's not one to be rash. Did you mention the name 'Kal'tsit' to the Rhodes Islanders?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Yes. They said that Kal'tsit previously served as a physician at Rhodes Island, but had died in an accident.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Are you certain?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I've dispatched a unit to the scene of the incident to ascertain. From what we know so far, there is no reason to think otherwise.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: <p=1>Wilderness, Central Governorate, Ursus</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: So... Kal'tsit is dead.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: The name that Chairman Witte extracted from the Chernobog lab files is no longer of use.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Because of the Sarcophagus, of course. Ursus needs its energy.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: I don't know the specifics, unfortunately.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.options: But Witte gave you a name: Kal'tsit.;He wanted you to find Kal'tsit through us.

attributes.content: Of course. Otherwise, he wouldn't have sent me alone—
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: ...Sent me to the Far North alone.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Yes, he trusts me. It's the duty of a soldier to follow orders, and it's not my place to complain, even if I should die in the Far North.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: But it shouldn't be this way. Miners and civilians were massacred, but the Grand Duchess gets out unscathed, and the Army is able to deploy a stone's throw from Grypherburg with no consequences.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: This isn't how things should be! How can I go back like this...?
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.options: If I told you, there was a way to bring Kal'tsit back to life...;What would you do?

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Are you joking? How is that possible? Is this Kal'tsit like the fae of legend, able to return to life whenever she wants?
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Wait a moment. The Chairman did say that the secret police once believed Director Kal'tsit to have died in that incident...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: But...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: You just tell me like that? Did you think I would trust you the same way in return? You're not even Ursine—
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: What if some of us used to be?
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: You...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Even if you really do know nothing, we won't forget that you once stood with us there, helping evacuate the Infected.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: We appreciate what you did, Lieutenant Gorchekova, even if you don't need our thanks. Am I right?
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Tell me, and I'll listen, once again as a child of Ursus.
attributes.name: Raidian

attributes.content: Alright, I knew I couldn't resist the chance to awaken a straying Ursine's identity.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: What I know about the Sarcophagus is very limited...
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: —Lieutenant?
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: I'm sorry. I think I got distracted.
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: It's alright. Bad habits of the elderly. I can't stop myself when I start talking about the motherland and our future with the youth...
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Perhaps you really do need rest. I've taken up too much of your time.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: No, Your High Excellency, I was simply thinking about what you said—
attributes.name: Eloysius

attributes.content: Get some rest, Lieutenant.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.content: Unlike the Far North, Grypherburg still sees some daylight this time of year. Do not forget to finish your report, once you've rested.
attributes.name: Witte

attributes.text: 'As the mines begin to reopen gradually in the Northern Tundra, the Third Army casts its gaze towards the Central Governorate.'
'The Fourth Army continues its illegal presence on the border of the Central Governorate. All citizens are advised to respect the cordon and refrain from traveling to the Lake Neva area.'

'The First Army has begun to conscript students from dozens of universities in the Central Governorate, without authorization from the State Council. Student protests across the country are being bloodily suppressed.'
'The Second and Seventh Armies have been mobilized, in response to Grypherburg's request...'

attributes.content: That's all you see in the papers these days. I've got a cousin going to school in the Central Governorate. Haven't heard from her in a while.
attributes.name: Student A

attributes.content: Did you hear? Uchitelnitsa Koshelna didn't go to Deity Grypherburg Imperial University. She went to the mines to teach the miners how to read!
attributes.name: Student B

attributes.content: That's so brave and selfless of her. I want to follow in her footsteps when I graduate...
attributes.name: Student B

attributes.content: I hope we'll see her again.
attributes.name: Student B

attributes.content: Don't listen to everything you read in the papers! It's all bad news to make us anxious and demoralize us.
attributes.name: Student C

attributes.content: The student council is holding a protest in Igor Memorial Park. Are you going?
attributes.name: Student C

attributes.content: Of course!
attributes.name: Student B

attributes.content: M-Me too!
attributes.name: Student A

attributes.content: There's a lot of movement in the military. Student and worker strikes are taking place all across the country, but they're being suppressed even swifter and more harshly than before...
attributes.name: Rosa

attributes.content: Also, the Grypherburg police recently arrested a student from the Literature Department attempting to graffiti the grand hall of the State Council. I heard the Petrachevsky Group is soliciting donations and trying to get him released.
attributes.name: Rosa

attributes.content: It's true, I checked with them. Every underground tabloid is spreading the news about him, and the words he wrote on the walls of the council hall.
attributes.name: Absinthe

attributes.content: The underground tabloids are printing far quicker than the censors can keep up.
attributes.name: Absinthe

attributes.content: Let's put that in the next letter to Sonya.
attributes.name: Rosa

attributes.content: They should be on their way back to Grypherburg, come to think of it...
attributes.name: Rosa

attributes.content: Write anyway. There are curfews everywhere. There's no guarantee they'll make it back on schedule.
attributes.name: Absinthe

attributes.content: Yes... I hope things go smoothly on their side.
attributes.name: Rosa

attributes.content: They will.
attributes.name: Absinthe

attributes.content: Getting in wasn't that hard.
attributes.name: Worker?

attributes.content: Just because I hacked the monitors doesn't mean you can let your guard down. They say the session has been suspended indefinitely, but they could also resume at any time.
attributes.name: Voice in Earpiece

attributes.content: The corridor leading to the meeting hall is still being patrolled four times an hour. Not counting the time it takes to get out, you have eight minutes to leave your mark.
attributes.name: Voice in Earpiece

attributes.content: Pee? Break something and take a piece?
attributes.name: Worker?

attributes.content: ...Can you do something that doesn't make so much noise and actually means something?
attributes.name: Voice in Earpiece

attributes.content: Well... I'll think about it.
attributes.name: Worker?

attributes.content: Why do I have to dress like this, anyway? It's not like my normal clothes are all that conspicuous.
attributes.name: Worker?

attributes.content: You're wearing a Grypherburg plumber's uniform. They don't have a seat on the council, but they have access to the council hall, and they're responsible for maintenance work throughout the building.
attributes.name: Voice in Earpiece

attributes.content: While there's no way for students like us—complete non-producers—to gain access to the council, much less gain any real influence.
attributes.name: Voice in Earpiece

attributes.content: Bah, why am I even explaining all this to you? I'm hanging up. You have six minutes.
attributes.name: Voice in Earpiece

attributes.content: Botani out.
attributes.name: Voice in Earpiece

attributes.content: Lokh.
attributes.name: Worker?

attributes.content: What should I do? Six minutes...
attributes.name: Worker?

attributes.content: The student in the plumber's uniform casts his gaze towards the end of the council hall, where the symbol of Ursine divine glory shines bright on the wall.
He thinks for a moment, then squints and pulls out a can of red spraypaint.
Brekin is not on duty today, but his colleague, who lives next door to him, sent his youngest son to his place and asked him to take his shift today, because he had a fever and a splitting headache.
Brekin, being the kind-hearted man he is, agreed. Scarcely could he imagine what he would see when he walked into the council hall—
Gosudar-batyushka—
attributes.name: Cleaner

attributes.content: The golden symbol on the wall is fully covered in red paint. Beneath it are words written with the same red paint, in the Ursine language—

attributes.text: <color=#FF4200>'The victor displays the emblem of the vanquished for his vanity, but he will forever stand in the shadow of the vanquished, if he cannot cast it away.'</color>
<color=#FF4200>—Yakov Petrov, penned in prison in the autumn of 1076</color>
